theme: The office should breathe with the team — every element reacts, every idle moment tells a story
visual_observations: Review #35 observations still hold. Carlos just shipped the whiteboard progress thermometer (#34.2) and the wall clock (#34.1). The cactus flower is still invisible. Mobile dead zone persists. Wander path overlap for 5+ idle workers still visible. Away signs remain static regardless of idle duration. The window sky gradient doesn't respond to team vibe. Previous review #35 had 7 detailed ideas — checking which ones are still unimplemented and which new angles emerge from the recent thermometer/clock additions.

ideas.title: Mobile Viewport Fill — Kill the Dead Zone
ideas.description: Still unimplemented from review #35. The mobile view (375×812) has ~370px of empty dark space below the office floor. The .office-floor uses aspect-ratio: 4/3 with no mobile override, locking the floor to ~281px tall. Fix: @media (max-width: 600px) { .office-floor { aspect-ratio: unset; min-height: calc(100dvh - 180px); } } — this fills the viewport. All internal elements use percentage positioning so they scale. Additionally reduce .office-wrap padding to 0.5rem on mobile. WHY: Phone users see a tiny box in darkness and bounce. This is a 4-line CSS fix that transforms the mobile experience.

ideas.title: Window Vibe Tint — The Sky Reflects Team Energy
ideas.description: Still unimplemented from review #35. The office window at right:2%, top:40% only reacts to time-of-day. Add a vibe-reactive overlay inside the window element. Add a sibling div .window-vibe-overlay with position:absolute, inset:0, mix-blend-mode:color, pointer-events:none. CSS vibe states: [data-vibe='on-fire'] → rgba(239,68,68,0.55) red sky, [data-vibe='crushing'] → rgba(34,197,94,0.4) green, [data-vibe='slow-day'] → rgba(156,163,175,0.35) grey wash. Transition: 1.4s ease. Pass vibeKey prop to the window render section in Office.jsx. WHY: The window is the most visually dominant element — making it read vibe creates instant ambient feedback without reading any text.

ideas.title: Wander Fan-Out — Idle Workers Don't Overlap
ideas.description: Still unimplemented from review #35. With 3+ idle workers, wander paths overlap because only 4 keyframe paths exist and workers 5+ trace identical paths. Add wander-5 through wander-8 as horizontal mirror-images of wander-1 through wander-4 (x values reflected around 50%, y values identical but offset 4-8% lower). Change wanderIdx from (i%4)+1 to (i%8)+1 in Office.jsx. WHY: Multiple idle workers should spread across the floor, not pile into a cluster.

ideas.title: Away Sign Duration Escalation — Notes Age With Time
ideas.description: Still unimplemented from review #35. Away signs currently cycle the same 6 messages regardless of how long someone's been idle. Add duration overrides: >=90min → '🏠 WFH maybe?', >=45min → '🍕 Still at lunch', >=25min → '📞 Long call...'. Add .desk--long-idle class when idleMinutes>=45 with filter:sepia(0.35) opacity(0.75) and a subtle curl animation. On-fire vibe override: all away signs show '🚨 NEEDED !!' in red. WHY: Time-aware away signs turn a static decoration into a narrative clock.

ideas.title: Clock Chime — The Wall Clock Announces the Hour
ideas.description: NEW idea building on the recently shipped wall clock (#34.1). The clock is purely visual. Add a subtle CSS animation that fires on the hour: when minutes === 0 (checked in the existing time tick), add a .clock--chime class for 3 seconds. CSS: .clock--chime { animation: clock-chime 0.3s ease 3; } @keyframes clock-chime { 0%,100%{transform:rotate(0)} 25%{transform:rotate(2deg)} 75%{transform:rotate(-2deg)} } — the clock wobbles 3 times like a gentle chime. At noon (12:00) and midnight (0:00), add .clock--big-chime with 5 wobbles and slightly larger rotation (4deg). WHY: A clock that chimes on the hour is a delightful surprise — you glance at the tab and catch it wobbling. It rewards people who keep the tab open.

ideas.title: Cactus Vibe Barometer — The Hidden Plant That Blooms
ideas.description: From review #35, still unimplemented. The cactus SVG has a yellow flower circle that's invisible because it's tiny and static. Extract cactus into OfficeCactus({vibeKey}) component. CSS: [data-vibe='crushing'] .cactus-flower { animation: cactus-bloom 1.4s ease infinite; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 4px rgba(251,191,36,0.9)); } — flower pulses and glows on crushing days. On-fire: flower turns red. After-hours: flower becomes indigo moonflower. WHY: A living plant that blooms when the team is thriving is an easter egg that rewards careful observers.

ideas.title: After-Hours Phantom — A Ghost Crosses the Dark Office
ideas.description: From review #35, still unimplemented. When vibe=after-hours, render a translucent, blurred silhouette of a random team member slowly crossing the floor every 3 minutes. CSS: .char--phantom { opacity:0; filter: blur(1.5px) grayscale(0.9) brightness(0.6); animation: phantom-walk 9s linear forwards; pointer-events:none; } @keyframes phantom-walk { 0%{left:-6%;opacity:0} 10%{opacity:0.12} 88%{opacity:0.12} 100%{left:106%;opacity:0} }. Pick ghost by (hourSeed % roster.length). WHY: A ghostly colleague crossing a dark office at midnight is a moment that makes someone screenshot and share. It transforms after-hours from 'screensaver' to 'short film'.
